prompt:
Ukiyo-e woodblock print composition. A Kabuki theatre interior viewed from 
a raked angle. Dozens of audience members fill the space — every one of 
them rendered as flat simplified silhouettes in pale ochre and soft grey, 
minimal line work, faces as blank ovals, no individual detail. One actor 
at center stage is rendered in full Ukiyo-e precision: bold variable-weight 
black ink outlines, elaborate kimono with geometric mon pattern in flat 
beni red and ai indigo blue, fully detailed face with white oshiroi makeup, 
precise hand gesture. The rendering contrast between the detailed actor and 
the undifferentiated audience is stark and immediate. Flat unmodulated color 
within all outlines. Visible woodgrain texture in solid color areas. Sky-area 
bokashi gradient in pale blue above the stage. Asymmetric composition, 
subject positioned at left-third. No Western atmospheric perspective. 
Muted palette: beni red, ai indigo, ki ochre, pale grey, black. 
No photographic shading. No gradients except background bokashi. 
No text, no legible labels, blank cartouche area.

Negative: western perspective, cast shadows, photographic gradients, 
full color spectrum, serif fonts, text, modern elements, 3D shading, 
airbrush effects, realistic anatomy
tradition_approach:
Flat unmodulated color within bold variable-weight 
  outlines; hierarchical figure detail (not size) encodes attention weights; 
  woodgrain texture; beni/ai/ki palette; raked perspective without vanishing 
  point.
